items.title: Is McDonald's (MCD) Share Price Justified After Recent Gains And Mixed Valuation Signals
items.description: If you are wondering whether McDonald's current share price reflects its true worth, you are not alone. This article is designed to help you weigh up what you might actually be paying for. The stock last closed at US$330.80, with a 1.2% decline over the past week, a 1.1% gain over the last 30 days and returns of 9.1% year to date, 6.7% over 1 year, 34.8% over 3 years and 68.1% over 5 years. Recent headlines around McDonald's have focused on its brand strength, menu evolution and global...

items.title: Should You Hold on to DASH Stock Despite Its 32% Plunge in Six Months?
items.description: DoorDash stock plummets 32% in the trailing six months amid fierce competition and valuation concerns, despite its strong order growth and expanding partnerships support outlook.

items.title: Unique fast-food burger chain closes its final location
items.description: Having an original idea gives businesses a first-mover advantage, allowing them to beat competitors to the punch. However, innovation that appeals to a narrow audience risks not attracting enough customers. Complicating matters, customers' dining preferences are changing — for example, from ...

items.title: Will McDonald's 210M Loyalty Users Fuel Long-Term Growth?
items.description: MCD's loyalty program nears 210M active users across more than 70 markets, boosting engagement and sales as it targets 250M members by 2027.

items.title: Icertis Reports Record Year of New Business Growth
items.description: BELLEVUE, Wash., March 10, 2026--Icertis, the global leader in AI-native contract intelligence, today announced a record year of new business growth in 2025. Nine Fortune-ranked companies, including BMW and McDonald’s, became new Icertis customers, along with prominent U.S. public sector organizations like the Defense Logistics Agency. The company now supports one out of every five businesses in the Fortune Global 500.
